codeReview.summary: Implementation has fundamental mismatches with the actual mcp-language-server tool interface. The code uses incorrect tool names ('rename' vs 'rename_symbol'), calls non-existent tools ('workspace_diagnostics', 'document_symbols'), and uses wrong argument formats (textDocumentUri+position object vs filePath+line+column). These are blocking issues that will cause all LSP tool calls to fail at runtime.

issues.description: LspRenameTool calls 'rename' but the actual mcp-language-server exposes tool named 'rename_symbol'. The call to mcp_client.call_tool("rename", ...) will fail with 'tool not found' error.

LspWorkspaceDiagnosticsTool calls 'workspace_diagnostics' but the mcp-language-server (isaacphi/mcp-language-server) does not expose this tool. It only has: definition, references, hover, diagnostics, rename_symbol, edit_file.
LspDocumentSymbolsTool calls 'document_symbols' but this tool does not exist in mcp-language-server.

LspDefinitionTool and other tools use argument format {textDocumentUri, position: {line, column}} but the actual mcp-language-server expects {filePath, line, column} (1-indexed line/column, not 0-indexed).

Argument structure mismatch: Implementation passes {textDocument: {uri: ...}, position: ...} but mcp-language-server expects {filePath: string, line: number, column: number}. This will cause all LSP calls to fail.

LspPosition uses 0-indexed lines/columns but LSP specification uses 0-indexed (per JSON-RPC), so this may be correct - but contradicts what mcp-language-server appears to expect based on its Go code which shows 1-indexed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker searched for mcp-language-server but didn't verify the actual tool names and argument formats from the source code. The actual server (github.com/isaacphi/mcp-language-server) has different tool names: 'rename_symbol' not 'rename', no 'workspace_diagnostics' or 'document_symbols'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: WebFetch of tools.go shows: definition, references, hover, diagnostics, rename_symbol, edit_file. No workspace_diagnostics or document_symbols.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md mentions MCP but doesn't specify how to verify MCP server tool interfaces. Workers should verify against actual server capabilities, not assume.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No guidance in AGENTS.md for MCP server capability verification

sharedStateObservations.observation: mcp-language-server is not listed in services.yaml but is a required external service for this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml only has swell-daemon. mcp-language-server is a runtime dependency.

summary: FAIL - The implementation has critical mismatches with the actual mcp-language-server interface. Tool names don't match (rename vs rename_symbol), non-existent tools are called (workspace_diagnostics, document_symbols), and argument formats are incompatible. The code builds and tests pass because tests mock the MCP client, but at runtime these LSP tools will fail.
